name: Alaska Patties
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C, Gas mark 5).
2. Drain the can of salmon and flake the fish.
3. Mix the flaked salmon with cheese, onions, and red pepper. Set aside.
4. In a bowl, combine flour, beaten eggs, salt, and basil to form a soft dough.
5. Divide the dough into egg-sized pieces.
6. Knead each piece until smooth.
7. Roll the dough pieces on a lightly floured board to 2mm (1/10 inch) thick or through a pasta maker at settings 5 or 6 over 3-4 rollings.
8. Cut the pasta sheets into 3cm (1 1/2 inch) squares.
9. Place salmon filling on one corner of each square, then fold diagonally to form triangular patties.
10. Dry patties on silicone paper for 1 hour before cooking, turning over after 30 minutes to dry undersides.
11. Pour tomato soup into a casserole dish and add the salmon patties.
12. Cover with a lid and cook in the oven for 30 minutes.
13. Uncover, sprinkle with grated cheddar cheese, and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes.
14. Serve with salad.
Serves 4. Approx. 390 kcals per serving
